title: Myths and Legends of the Sioux
description: Myths and Legends of the Sioux — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Myths and Legends of the Sioux" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "Adoption"
memes.description: "Adoption is a recurring social event in Sioux tales — abandoned children, orphans, and strangers are taken into families (003-021, 022-067, 030-023), transforming outsiders into kin and weaving the broader social fabric."

memes.label: "Ambush"
memes.description: "The ambush — lying in wait to strike by surprise — was a tactical event in Sioux warfare, appearing both as physical combat strategy and as the treacherous method of assassination in the tales."

memes.label: Arikara
memes.description: A Native American tribe neighboring the Sioux, appearing in two origin stories — as the corn-gathering woman in The Forgotten Ear of Corn and as the petrified wife in the Legend of Standing Rock. (001-001)(012-001)

memes.label: "Arrival"
memes.description: "The arrival — whether at a village, a home, or a fateful destination — is a structurally significant event in Sioux narratives, often marking the turning point between journey and consequence, absence and reunion."

memes.label: "Arrowhead (Flint)"
memes.description: "Flint arrowheads in Sioux mythology — essential hunting tools made by Unktomi the spider, sourced from the flint body of the Bear Chief, and capable of both providing sustenance and dealing death."

memes.label: "The Bank"
memes.description: "The bank in Sioux tales is the steep edge of a river or creek — a place of repose, encounter, hidden dwelling, and sudden descent where characters lie down, find doors, and leap off."

memes.label: "Battle"
memes.description: "Battles were pivotal events in Sioux life — from intertribal warfare and war party skirmishes to the internal psychological battles of warriors. The field of battle was both a place of honor and death, where warriors proved their courage and spirits of the slain lingered."

memes.label: "Bear (Mato)"
memes.description: "The Bear of Sioux folklore — a powerful and often predatory figure who appears as both an adversary with a flint body and as Rabbit's unreliable hunting companion."

memes.label: "Big Eagle"
memes.description: "Big Eagle is a brave Sioux hunter who, while journeying with his wife and her mother, encounters a mysterious old man who leads him to discover a vast hidden encampment of his people."

memes.label: "Blood Clot"
memes.description: "In Sioux folklore, a clot of buffalo blood hidden by Rabbit transforms into a handsome youth who becomes his adopted son and avenges the wrongs done to him by a tyrannical bear (022-052–022-068)."

memes.label: "Brave (Sioux Warrior)"
memes.description: "In Sioux culture, a brave was a warrior who had demonstrated courage in combat — a social identity earned through brave deeds and recognized by the council and community (007-016)(024-009)(024-103)."

memes.label: "Buffalo"
memes.description: "The buffalo — central to Sioux subsistence, culture, and ceremony — providing meat, hides, and the sacred white buffalo cow of legend."

memes.label: "The Butte (Paha)"
memes.description: "The butte in Sioux territory — a distinctive elevated landform that serves as the setting for transformative events, including Unktomi's arrowhead workshop and the Mysterious Butte tale."

memes.label: "The Camp"
memes.description: "The camp in Sioux tales is the mobile community — a temporary village formed on journeys (004-030), moved at command (012-003), and centered around its fires (P02-053) where stories are told and decisions made."

memes.label: "Ceremonial Smoking"
memes.description: "Ceremonial smoking was a foundational Sioux social and spiritual practice — the shared pipe before council, the peace smoke sealing alliances, and the ritual smoking accompanying prayers and offerings."

memes.label: "Chase"
memes.description: "The chase — a pursuit driven by hunger, anger, or sport — appears in Sioux tales as a test of speed, endurance, and cunning, distinct from the organized hunt in its urgency and unpredictability."

memes.label: "Chaske (First Born)"
memes.description: "Chaske (meaning 'first born'), son of a great war chief — a noble and generous young warrior who chose friendship over status, gave up his love for his friend Hake, and proved his bravery on the warpath."

memes.label: Chippewa
memes.description: A neighboring Native American tribe frequently mentioned in Sioux oral traditions, appearing as both rivals in warfare and participants in inter-tribal relations. (028-041–028-044)

memes.label: "Corn (Wagmiza)"
memes.description: "Corn in Sioux culture — a sacred gift and agricultural staple appearing in origin stories, planting rituals, and tales of divine provision throughout the legendary cycle."

memes.label: "The Council (Oyate)"
memes.description: "The tribal council in Sioux culture — the governance gathering where leaders deliberate, justice is dispensed, and community decisions are made, appearing across multiple tales as the mechanism of collective authority."

memes.label: "Courtship Customs"
memes.description: "Sioux courtship and marriage customs — parental consent, the suitor's approach, gift exchange, and the dynamics of taking a wife."

memes.label: "Crawfish"
memes.description: "The Crawfish of Sioux folklore — a cautious scavenger who nearly becomes the Raccoon's meal when he investigates what appears to be a dead animal, but whose wariness saves him from the trick."

memes.label: "The Creek"
memes.description: "Creeks in Sioux tales are places of rest, concealment, and the supernatural — a traveler drinks at a little creek (020-154), a ghost haunts a thickly wooded creek (030-119), and a creek winds through a beautiful valley (023-046)."

memes.label: "Dance Ceremony"
memes.description: "Dance and song as central elements of Sioux ceremony — from the sacred Sun Dance to war dances, courtship dances, and comic performances."

memes.label: "Dead Shot"
memes.description: "Dead Shot is a Sioux warrior who earned his title for his exceptional marksmanship — he who never misses his target. He is married to Beautiful Dove and appears in a tale involving White Plume."
aliases: He Who Never Misses His Mark

memes.label: "Death"
memes.description: "Death in Sioux tradition was shaped by the manner of dying — an honorable death in battle ensured swift passage to the spirit world, while a dishonorable death trapped the spirit. The death song, burial customs, and beliefs about the afterlife were central to warrior culture."

memes.label: "Dedication"
memes.description: "*In loving memory of my mother,*  *MARY GRAHAM BUISSON,*  *at whose knee most of the stories contained in this little vo"

memes.label: "Devils Lake"
memes.description: "A lake and historic Indian Agency in North Dakota — an important location in Sioux territory and the site where the author first encountered Sioux oral traditions."

memes.label: "Disappearance"
memes.description: "Mysterious disappearances recur throughout Sioux tales — characters vanish into clouds, forests, tepees, darkness, and the western sky, often marking supernatural intervention or transformation rather than ordinary departure."

memes.label: "Eagle"
memes.description: "The eagle occupied a paramount position in Sioux spirituality as a sacred bird, a hunting target for aspiring warriors, and the source of the most prestigious feathers. The red eagle appeared as a powerful spirit-being in the White Plume narrative, driving away all other birds from the land."

memes.label: "Earth (Mother Earth)"
memes.description: "In Sioux cosmology, the Earth is a sacred maternal entity — Mother Earth (Maka Ina) — revered alongside the Great Mystery as a life-giving force, invoked in prayers and ceremonies, and deeply connected to creation and burial customs."

memes.label: "Elk (Hehaka)"
memes.description: "The Elk of Sioux folklore — a proud animal who attempts to play a trick on Rabbit by pretending to be caught in a bowstring, only to become Rabbit's prize catch through his own foolish jest."

memes.label: "The Feast (Wicohan)"
memes.description: "The feast in Sioux culture — a ceremonial gathering that marks significant events, seals alliances, and reinforces community bonds across the legendary cycle, from hunting celebrations to peace councils."

memes.label: "Feather"
memes.description: "Feathers held deep symbolic meaning in Sioux culture as marks of war honors, spiritual protection, and ceremonial adornment. Warriors 'earned their feathers' through deeds, and feathers adorned sacred objects, painted arrows, and the regalia of respected figures."

memes.label: "Fight"
memes.description: "Individual fights — as distinct from large-scale battles — recur in Sioux tales as tests of personal courage, strength, and character, ranging from wrestling matches to life-or-death struggles between named opponents."

memes.label: "Fire"
memes.description: "Fire held central importance in Sioux daily and ceremonial life — from campfire cooking and warmth to ritual fire-building, ash preservation, and fire's role in storytelling and social gatherings."

memes.label: "Flint (Arrowhead Material)"
memes.description: "Flint in Sioux culture — the essential material for arrowheads and tools, symbolizing both practical craftsmanship and invulnerability in folklore, as seen in the Bear with the Flint Body."

memes.label: "The Forest (Deep Woods)"
memes.description: "The deep forest in Sioux tales is the place of concealment, solitude, and supernatural encounter — a man disappears into the forest (020-068), a hunter wanders studying its plants (026-005), and strange noises emerge from its darkness (034-043)."

memes.label: "Foreword"
memes.description: "In publishing these “Myths of the Sioux,” I deem it proper to state that

memes.label: "Funeral"
memes.description: "In Sioux tradition, funerals involved placing the deceased on an elevated burial scaffold, followed by the family giving away their possessions and entering a period of mourning marked by cutting their hair and wearing poor apparel (033-009–033-018)."

memes.label: "Gathering"
memes.description: "Gatherings in Sioux tales range from the practical — gathering corn from the field (001-001) or wood for fire (037-013) — to the ceremonial — large gatherings that draw crowds (031-075) — marking moments where individuals become community."

memes.label: "Ghost (Wanagi)"
memes.description: "The ghost (wanagi) in Sioux belief — the spirit of a deceased person, which could be released through ceremony, wander as a restless presence when death was dishonorable, or manifest as a Wakan (holy) being that haunted the living."

memes.label: "The Gift of the Peace Pipe"
memes.description: "The sacred event in which a divine buffalo woman appeared to two Sioux men and gave the people the Peace Pipe — establishing the foundation for all future Sioux ceremonies and treaties."

memes.label: "Great Spirit"
memes.description: "The supreme spiritual entity in Sioux belief — the Great Spirit (Wakan Tanka) to whom prayers, ceremonies, and offerings are directed."

memes.label: "The Grouse Girls"
memes.description: "A flock of grouse maidens who are tricked into a bag by the Rabbit, but escape through the clever aid of the Rabbit's grandmother."

memes.label: "Hake (Last Born)"
memes.description: "Hake (meaning 'last born'), an orphan raised by his grandmother — chosen by Chaske as his lifelong friend, he wins the love of Pretty Feather and proves his courage on the warpath."

memes.label: "The Old Hermit"
memes.description: "An old medicine man who lived alone in the forest, discovered corn for the Sioux people, and taught them how to cache provisions underground."

memes.label: "The Hill"
memes.description: "Hills in Sioux tales serve as lookouts and landmarks — a village sits 'on high land overlooking a lake' (016-002), warriors watch from hills (024-048), and children coast down hills on boards (006-004) — the hill is the landscape's punctuation, giving shape and vantage to the flat prairie."

memes.label: "Hunt"
memes.description: "The hunt was a central communal event in Sioux life — the buffalo hunt mobilized entire villages, the fall hunt replenished winter stores, and the solitary hunt tested individual skill. Hunting was simultaneously an economic necessity, a social ritual, and a rite of passage for young men."

memes.label: "Hunting Traditions"
memes.description: "Sioux hunting practices — the skills, rituals, and social organization surrounding the hunt, which provided the primary means of subsistence."

memes.label: "Journey"
memes.description: "Journeys structured Sioux narrative and life — from the village camping journey to the solitary hero's quest, journeys were events of transformation, discovery, and return. The long journey tested endurance and often served as the narrative frame within which adventures and encounters occurred."

memes.label: "The Knoll"
memes.description: "The knoll in Sioux tales is a small round hill — a place of play, lookout, and supernatural encounter where children race and mysterious movements occur."

memes.label: "The Lake"
memes.description: "Lakes in Sioux tales are places of drowning, transformation, and supernatural encounter — frogs leap into a lake to their death (019-015), a muskrat lives beneath its surface (008-003), and turtles gather at a sacred little lake (025-003)."

memes.label: "Little Brave"
memes.description: "Little Brave (simply called Brave in the original tale) is a mischievous young boy in Sioux folklore who plays a memorable prank on his playmates by pretending to be the risen corpse of a medicine woman buried in a hidden grave (016-018–016-020)."

memes.label: "The Lodge (Tipi)"
memes.description: "The lodge (tipi) in Sioux culture — the primary dwelling and domestic space, serving as the backdrop for family life, council gatherings, and supernatural encounters throughout the legendary cycle."

memes.label: "The Lone Warrior"
memes.description: "The Lone Warrior is a Sioux brave who earned his name by going on the warpath alone, returning with spoils that he generously distributed among the poor families of his village."
aliases: The Brave Who Went on the Warpath Alone

memes.label: "Medicine Man"
memes.description: "A spiritual leader and healer in Sioux society who uses sacred medicine, roots, and rituals to cure the sick and interpret omens."

memes.label: "The Medicine Woman"
memes.description: "The Medicine Woman was a healer or conjurer buried long ago near a Sioux winter camp. Her grave, hidden by choke cherry bushes, became the site of a memorable prank when a mischievous boy named Brave pretended to be her risen spirit (016-012–016-014)."

memes.label: "Mourning"
memes.description: "Mourning in Sioux tales is a structured social event — hair is cut (003-022), mourning feasts are held (003-027), robes are drawn over heads (015-004), and communities are told to 'put aside your mourning' (033-020) after a prescribed period, marking grief as a collective ritual with a defined beginning and end."

memes.label: "Muskrat"
memes.description: "The Muskrat is a personified animal character in Sioux folklore who engages in a contest of pride with the Artichoke, each boasting of their virtues and the esteem in which they are held."

memes.label: "The Only Daughter"
memes.description: "The Only Daughter is a central figure in a Sioux tale of death and resurrection — a beautiful maiden who refuses all suitors, dies suddenly, and is miraculously brought back to life through the intervention of a determined young man."
aliases: the maiden who refused all suitors

memes.label: "Paint (Body and Face Painting)"
memes.description: "Body and face painting was a vital cultural practice among the Sioux, serving as a visual language for mourning, war preparation, beauty, and ritual identity. Red paint signaled either beauty or battle-readiness, while unpainted faces marked grief, and painted robes conferred honor."

memes.label: "Peace Pipe (Chanunpa)"
memes.description: "A sacred ceremonial pipe given to the Sioux by a divine buffalo woman — used to seal treaties and perform all ceremonies."

memes.label: "The Pet Crane"
memes.description: "The Pet Crane is a young crane rescued and raised by a hunter's family in Sioux folklore. When game vanishes and the family faces starvation, the crane's father brings fat to sustain them; later, the pet crane gives its life to deceive a Crow war party, saving the family that raised it (034-082–034-084)."

memes.label: "The Prairie"
memes.description: "The prairie in Sioux tales is the open, exposed land — the place of long journeys (023-134), winter hardship (006-001), and the great buffalo herds. It is both the domain of the prairie wolf (034-061) and the frozen expanse where travelers risk death (030-134)."

memes.label: "Pretty Feather"
memes.description: "Pretty Feather is the beautiful daughter of an old medicine man in Sioux folklore, loved by both Chaske and his friend Hake. Her choice of the orphan Hake over the chief's son Chaske sets in motion a tale of friendship, sacrifice, and the warrior's path to honor. (030-036–030-054)"

memes.label: "Pretty Feathered Forehead (Pretty Feather)"
memes.description: "Pretty Feathered Forehead (commonly called Pretty Feather) is a central female character in Sioux folklore — the beloved of Chaske and the focus of a courtship tale involving two young friends and a test of loyalty."

memes.label: "Punishment"
memes.description: "Punishment in Sioux tales operates as supernatural justice — Rabbit's brother places wrongdoers in suspended torment (020-059, 020-074, 020-109, 020-133), while tribal punishment is threatened for violations of custom (017-061)."

memes.label: "Rabbit (Mastincala)"
memes.description: "The Rabbit trickster of Sioux folklore — a mischievous and clever character who appears in at least seven tales, outwitting giants, bears, and wolves, and famously losing his tail."

memes.label: "Raccoon (Spotted Face)"
memes.description: "The Raccoon — called Spotted Face by the Sioux — a cunning trickster who outwits the crawfish by playing dead, then turns their feast into his own."

memes.label: "The Ravine (Coulee)"
memes.description: "In Sioux tales, the ravine is a place of sudden encounter and concealment — a beautiful woman rises from a ravine (013-002), a man climbs through a deep ravine (023-055), and warriors leave markers in the next ravine (030-183)."

memes.label: "The River"
memes.description: "Rivers in Sioux tales are places of transformation, grief, and supernatural encounter — lovers drown themselves in rivers (007-089), water is drawn at the riverbank (014-004), and characters follow rivers on journeys of fate."

memes.label: "The Burial Scaffold"
memes.description: "The burial scaffold in Sioux tales is a raised platform for the dead — a place of grief, transformation, and supernatural encounter where springs burst forth and spirits linger."

memes.label: "Scalping (Scalp Taking)"
memes.description: "Scalp taking was the warrior's proof of combat victory — the physical trophy that verified a kill. Scalps were displayed at war dances (023-165), taken as revenge (030-167), and sought as the purpose of war parties (007-014)."

memes.label: "The Shore"
memes.description: "The shore in Sioux tales is a boundary between water and land — a place of arrival, departure, transformation, and encounter where characters emerge from the depths or gather to sun themselves."

memes.label: "The Simpleton"
memes.description: "The Simpleton of Sioux folklore — a seemingly foolish young man whose unconventional wisdom saves a mourning mother from self-destruction and restores her to life through unexpected insight."

memes.label: Sioux
memes.description: The Sioux (Dakota) are the Native American people whose myths, legends, and folk-lore are preserved in Marie L. McLaughlin's 1916 collection. The stories were told by Sioux elders at lodges and camp fires across the Dakota territories (P02-052).

memes.label: "Song and Singing"
memes.description: "Song permeated Sioux life as the voice of love, death, humor, and ceremony. Warriors sang their death songs while charging into battle, lovers crooned melodies to woo, and comic songs lightened communal gatherings — song was the emotional register of the culture."

memes.label: "Spirit World"
memes.description: "The Sioux conception of the spirit world — dwelling place of the dead, domain of spirits and ghosts, and realm of the Great Spirit (Wakan Tanka)."

memes.label: "Standing Rock"
memes.description: "A sacred stone formation on the Standing Rock Sioux Reservation — believed by the Sioux to be a woman turned to stone, and revered as wakan (holy)."

memes.label: "The Standing Rock Transformation"
memes.description: "The legendary event in which a jealous Arikara woman was turned to stone — giving rise to the sacred Standing Rock and the name of the Standing Rock Sioux Reservation."
aliases: The Woman Who Turned to Stone

memes.label: "Stealing (Theft)"
memes.description: "Stealing in Sioux tales encompasses both the heroic — horse-stealing raids that demonstrate warrior prowess — and the transgressive — stealing a bride (013-006) or another's property — marking the boundary between honorable acquisition and crime."

memes.label: "Stone Boy (Inyanhoksila)"
memes.description: "Stone Boy (Inyanhoksila) of Sioux folklore — the youngest of four orphan brothers who, after being killed by a water monster, is resurrected as a stone being with supernatural strength and becomes the hero of his family."

memes.label: "The Pet Rabbit"
memes.description: "A little girl owned a pet rabbit which she loved dearly."

memes.label: "The Pet Donkey"
memes.description: "There was a chief’s daughter once who had a great many relations so that

memes.label: "The Rabbit and the Grouse Girls"
memes.description: "The rabbit once went out on the prairie in winter time."

memes.label: "The Faithful Lovers"
memes.description: "There once lived a chief’s daughter who had many relations."

memes.label: "The Artichoke and the Muskrat"

memes.label: "The Rabbit, and the Bear with the Flint Body"
memes.description: "The Rabbit and his grandmother were in dire straits, because the rabbit

memes.label: "The Raccoon and the Crawfish"
memes.description: "Sharp and cunning is the raccoon, say the Indians, by whom he is named

memes.label: "Legend of Standing Rock"
memes.description: "A Dakota had married an Arikara woman, and by her had one child."

memes.label: "Story of the Peace Pipe"
memes.description: "Two young men were out strolling one night talking of love affairs."

memes.label: "A Bashful Courtship"
memes.description: "A young man lived with his grandmother."

memes.label: "The Simpleton's Wisdom"
memes.description: "There was a man and his wife who had one daughter."

memes.label: "Little Brave and the Medicine Woman"
memes.description: "A village of Indians moved out of winter camp and pitched their tents

memes.label: "The Bound Children"
memes.description: "There once lived a widow with two children--the elder a daughter and the

memes.label: "The Signs of Corn"
memes.description: "When corn is to be planted by the Indians, it is the work of the women

memes.label: "Unktomi and the Arrowheads"
memes.description: "There were once upon a time two young men who were very great friends,

memes.label: "The Bear and the Rabbit Hunt Buffalo"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there lived as neighbors, a bear and a rabbit."

memes.label: "The Sioux Who Married the Crow Chief's Daughter"

memes.label: "The Boy and the Turtles"
memes.description: "A boy went on a turtle hunt, and after following the different streams

memes.label: "The Hermit, or the Gift of Corn"
memes.description: "In a deep forest, far from the villages of his people, lived a hermit."

memes.label: "The Mysterious Butte"
memes.description: "A young man was once hunting and came to a steep hill."

memes.label: "The Wonderful Turtle"
memes.description: "Near to a Chippewa village lay a large lake, and in this lake there

memes.label: "The Man and the Oak"
memes.description: "There once lived a Sioux couple who had two children, a boy and a girl."

memes.label: "Story of the Two Young Friends"
memes.description: "There were once in a very large Indian camp two little boys who were

memes.label: "The Story of the Pet Crow"
memes.description: "Once upon a time there came to a large village a plague of crows."

memes.label: "The Resuscitation of the Only Daughter"
memes.description: "There once lived an old couple who had an only daughter."

memes.label: "The Story of the Pet Crane"
memes.description: "There was once upon a time a man who did not care to live with his tribe

memes.label: "White Plume"
memes.description: "There once lived a young couple who were very happy."

memes.label: "Story of Pretty Feathered Forehead"
memes.description: "There was once a baby boy who came into the world with a small cluster

memes.label: "The Four Brothers or Inyanhoksila (Stone Boy)"
memes.description: "Alone and apart from their tribe dwelt four orphan brothers."

memes.label: "The Unktomi (Spider), Two Widows and the Red Plums"
memes.description: "There once lived, in a remote part of a great forest, two widowed

memes.label: "Storytelling"
memes.description: "Sioux oral storytelling tradition — tales told in the lodges to explain the natural world, preserve history, and pass cultural knowledge through generations."

memes.label: "Thunder Beings"
memes.description: "The Thunder Beings (Wakinyan) — powerful sky spirits of Sioux cosmology who manifest as thunder and lightning, wielding destructive and protective power over the world below."

memes.label: "The Trail"
memes.description: "The trail in Sioux narratives is the visible track left by people, game, or supernatural beings — followed by pursuers, hunters, and heroes across prairie and through timber, marking movement and intent across the landscape."

memes.label: Trick
memes.description: "Trickery is a central motif in Sioux tales — from Rabbit's elaborate ruses to Unktomi's schemes, the trick drives plot and reveals character through cunning, deception, and the unexpected reversal."

memes.label: "Turtle (Keya)"
memes.description: "The Turtle of Sioux and Chippewa folklore — a wise and prudent figure who serves as judge for the tribe, marries the chief's daughter, and outwits his mockers with clever stories."

memes.label: "The Two Widows"
memes.description: "The Two Widows are a pair of sisters in Sioux folklore who, while caring for their infant children, are tricked by Unktomi (Spider) into pursuing a mirage of red plums — a cautionary tale about the cunning of the trickster."

memes.label: "Unktomi (Spider)"
memes.description: "The Spider trickster of Sioux folklore — a cunning and sometimes dangerous figure who appears in multiple tales, making arrowheads and pursuing the Pemmican Man."

memes.label: "The Valley"
memes.description: "In Sioux tales, valleys appear as places of beauty, shelter, and vantage — threaded by creeks (023-046), overlooked from surrounding hills (024-048), and traversed by figures on long journeys (037-083)."

memes.label: "The Village"
memes.description: "The village (otunwahe) in Sioux tales is the primary social space — the place of rejoicing (004-004), departure (004-030), return (024-083), and collective action where 'all the village women' (004-004) and 'the whole village' (010-003) act as one."

memes.label: Wakan
memes.description: The Lakota concept of the holy, sacred, or spiritually powerful — a fundamental category in Sioux belief encompassing spirits, holy persons, and sacred objects. (030-123)(031-035)(035-068)

memes.label: War Party
memes.description: "War parties in Sioux tales are organized groups of warriors conducting raids, reconnaissance, and inter-tribal warfare — central to male identity and the social organization of conflict."

memes.label: "Warrior Culture"
memes.description: "The Sioux warrior tradition — a code of bravery, honor, and skill in battle that defined male social status and community leadership."

memes.label: "Wasna (Pemmican)"
memes.description: "A traditional Sioux food made of dried buffalo meat pounded into a paste with fat and berries — a staple provision for hunting and war parties."

memes.label: "White Plume"
memes.description: "The hero son of Dead Shot and Beautiful Dove — born under a white plume sign, he grows up to become a great warrior and adventurer in Sioux legend."

memes.label: "Wolf"
memes.description: "The wolf in Sioux narratives appears as a powerful other-than-human being — a chief of a wolf village, a howling scout, a shape-shifter, and a creature of the hunt. Wolves walked the boundary between human society and the wild."
